mnemonics.title: IPv4 Header Fields Order
mnemonics.content: VIITDUTFS (Version, IHL, Identification, Total Length, TTL, Protocol, Source Address, Destination Address, Options) - Say 'VEE IT DUTT FESS' while reading top to bottom on a packet capture

acronyms.usage: Used to calculate header size - value × 4 = bytes
Hop counter to prevent infinite loops
Maximum packet size for a network link
Used for error reporting (e.g., fragmentation needed)
Transport protocol with Protocol field value 17

visualTips.visualization: Draw a 32-bit wide row structure: [Version 4][IHL 4][TOS 8][Total Length 16] = Row 1; [Identification 16][Flags 3][Fragment Offset 13] = Row 2; [TTL 8][Protocol 8][Header Checksum 16] = Row 3; [Source IP Address 32] = Row 4; [Destination IP Address 32] = Row 5; [Options (variable)] = Row 6+

Picture a large envelope (datagram) that must go through a smaller mailbox (MTU). You cut it into multiple smaller letters (fragments), each with same return address (Identification) and page numbers (Fragment Offset)
Draw all 16-bit words in a column, perform binary addition with carry wrapping around, then take one's complement (invert all bits) for final checksum

analogies.concept: IPv4 Header as a Postal Letter
analogies.analogy: Like a letter with sender address (Source IP), recipient address (Destination IP), priority marker (TOS), tracking number (Identification), and 'do not fold' instruction (DF flag)

analogies.concept: Fragmentation as Dividing a Book Chapter
analogies.analogy: A large chapter (IP datagram) must be sent in multiple mailings. Each mailing has chapter number (Identification), page numbers (Fragment Offset), and 'more pages coming' note (MF flag)

analogies.concept: TTL as a Meeting Invitation Expiry
analogies.analogy: An invitation that gets stamped at each office (router). After N stamps (TTL), it's thrown away. This prevents invitations from circulating forever if they can't find the right person
